title: Sabalenka Advances in Miami Open with Eye on Sunshine Double
summary: Aryna Sabalenka, the world number one tennis player, advanced to the last 16 of the Miami Open by defeating Caty McNally 6-4, 6-2. This victory extends her winning streak to eight matches as she pursues the 'Sunshine Double,' aiming to become only the fifth woman to win both the Indian Wells and Miami tournaments in the same year. Sabalenka previously won Indian Wells this month. In the next round, she will face China's Zheng Qinwen, who recently returned from elbow surgery and beat a top-20 player. The article also covers other matches: Elena Rybakina won against Marta Kostyuk, Jessica Pegula defeated Leylah Fernandez, and Jelena Ostapenko overcame Jasmine Paolini. These results highlight the competitive nature of the Miami Open and the players' progress toward the championship.

background_read: The Miami Open is a prestigious WTA 1000 tennis tournament held annually in Miami, Florida, attracting top female players worldwide. Aryna Sabalenka, from Belarus, is the current world number one and defending champion, known for her powerful game. The 'Sunshine Double' refers to winning both the BNP Paribas Open in Indian Wells and the Miami Open in the same year, a rare feat achieved by only four women previously. This article provides updates on match results, player performances, and the tournament's progression, emphasizing Sabalenka's quest for this double victory and the competitive landscape involving other seeded players like Elena Rybakina and Jessica Pegula.
Article_Structure: Main Points: The article reports Aryna Sabalenka's victory over Caty McNally to advance to the last 16 of the Miami Open, highlighting her pursuit of the Sunshine Double after winning Indian Wells. It also covers other match results, including wins by Elena Rybakina, Jessica Pegula, and Jelena Ostapenko, showcasing the tournament's competitive dynamics. Purpose: To inform tennis fans about recent outcomes and player progress in the Miami Open, emphasizing Sabalenka's achievements and the broader context of the WTA tour. Evidence Evaluation: The article relies on factual match scores and player statistics, typical of sports reporting, but lacks in-depth analysis or external sources. Author Credibility: As a BBC Tennis article, it benefits from the BBC's reputation for reliable sports journalism, though the author is not specified. Methodology: Information is gathered from live scores and results, presented in a straightforward news format to update readers on tournament events.
